games.explanation: STL is the better team by Elo and the sim confirms it — the market isn't giving them enough credit. Michael McGreevy toes the rubber against Joe Boyle. Pitcher's park suppresses the long ball.

PHI is the better team by Elo and the sim confirms it — the market isn't giving them enough credit. Aaron Nola toes the rubber against Jacob deGrom. The sim projects PHI to plate 5.1 runs — expect some fireworks.
LAA is getting plus-money at +126 and the model thinks that's too cheap. Reid Detmers keeps LAA competitive on the mound against Cristian Javier. LAA holds a commanding 86-point Elo edge.

The market has ARI as a +205 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Eduardo Rodriguez keeps ARI competitive on the mound against Tyler Glasnow. The sim projects ARI to plate 5.5 runs — expect some fireworks.
The market has CLE as a +158 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Joseph Cantillo keeps CLE competitive on the mound against Bryan Woo. Pitcher's park suppresses the long ball.
The market has WSN as a +200 underdog, but our simulation disagrees. Miles Mikolas keeps WSN competitive on the mound against Cade Horton. Cold temps suppress the bats.
